Introduction to the Coaching The Conversion Meth... (Completed
05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 1 of 4
Speaker 1: Hey guys. Welcome back to module one of 10x Launches. In this lesson, we are actually
going to be going deeper into The Coaching The Conversion Method, and I really want to
just start off with a brief two-minute history of how this method came to be. It was
really a unique clash of circumstances that led to the insights and the breakthroughs and
really the implementation or necessity of writing copy, especially launch copy, using this
method.
So just to give you a little bit of context, it was really about two years ago when I was
hitting my stride as an in-demand launch copywriter, writing or consulting for some of
my own personal heroes and mentors and executing those massive six and seven-figure,
how the heck did they even pull those off type of launches. So that's really the time
period that I started needing to develop this.
As great as that was, that period of my career, the problem was I was also really hitting
my stride as a Hollywood screenwriter. It was totally by fluke and accident that projects
that I had been working on three or four or five years prior were finally starting to get
steam. So one project got auctioned by an award-winning producer, and we went
through the rounds and rewrites and getting directors involved and all the things that
happen when you have to get something production ready. In another instance, right off
the heels of that, I was hired to write a brand new screenplay from scratch for a
production company here in Canada.
So there was a lot going on. It was a hectic time, and this was actually further
complicated by the fact that I was also hitting my stride as a coach, what I called an
expression engineer. So during the peak of all this, I was running about 15 hours worth
of coaching sessions per week, really unpacking what it was I was blocking people from
living their fullest expression and helping them re-engineer their lives and realities to
really kind of support that full expression.
So all this, kind of the common denominator, was fueled by this deep, deep, deep kind
of fascination with what drives human behavior and catalyzes transformation, but I was
also equally inspired and influenced by marketing and persuasion legends like Eugene
Schwartz, Robert Cialdini, Robert Collier, as well as many personal growth legends and
psychologists and transformational leaders like Stephen Covey and Maxwell Maltz and
Viktor Frankl.
So all this was going on, and then finally here was kind of the clincher. I took "mind
blown" a bit too seriously. All of these things were coming right immediately off the
heels of having a stroke. I took a vacation from my last full-time job and on day two
woke up half paralyzed, and I recovered fully. Thankful for that, but coming out of that
and really recovering and having all these things going on, I had maybe about three or
four hours of focused productive time per day to get all these things done, which on the
surface made no sense. And something had to be cut, but at the same time there was
this very clear message that I had to live my own full expression, that I couldn't
amputate any of these interests in coaching or screenwriting or copywriting in order to
meet the demands, the growing demands, of each of them. I had to find a way to really
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 4 --

Introduction to the Coaching The Conversion Meth... (Completed
05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 2 of 4
embrace the slash between these roles, to integrate them, to leverage them
strategically, and finally really to bring all things to one thing.
And that's really what ignited this journey. It was to create a methodology or process
that would allow me to get exceptional results for clients in less time because I didn't
have all the time to do it, while also embracing all these aspects of myself that were
yearning for expression. And at the same time, it just turned out that they all synergized
much, much better than what I expected.
But really, what it all comes down to, there's been this question ever since I started my
marketing journey over a decade ago. The big question that's always fueled me was this.
Why do over 97% of qualified, perfectly targeted leads not buy? And more importantly,
why the heck do we settle for that? As coaches, educators, entrepreneurs, and program
creators who genuinely care about our audience, and I mean really deeply care about
them, why are we so willing to let over 97% of them go underserved? And what if,
instead of giving up on them, we made it not just our right but our responsibility to give
every single person who enters our funnel the best possible chance of becoming a
customer.
And the way I like to equate this is let's say you had 10 kids and only one or half of one
was open to receiving your advice and guidance. What would you do about the other
nine? Would you give up on them or would you do everything in your power to just
really deeply understand where they're at and do what you had to do to empathize, to
open them up, and to create a new context for them in which they are ready and
empowered to receive your guidance. So it's really the same level of care I want you to
bring to your audience.
And finally, this was all fueled by a very intense curiosity. Why is it that one-on-one or
collaborative selling, often done by coaches, can convert anywhere from 20% to 50%,
literally 10x what typical online marketing funnels can generate? And of course a biggie,
how can we start bridging that gap at scale?
So that really is the inquiry that has fueled this method, and that's where coaching the
conversion comes into play. It's really picking up where traditional direct response and
persuasion techniques have left us, with conversions of 1% to 3%, and bringing and
integrating and leveraging the mindsets, tools, and strategies that are used by some of
the world's best salespeople and coaches and therapists and leaders. People who
ethically influence behavior with positive and predictable outcomes. That is what we're
doing here.
And now here's the main premise that is underlying this whole process. This is based on
the fact that the version of someone who buys your product is fundamentally different
than the person who initially came into your sequence or funnel. And it's the job of the
copywriter or the conversion coach to understand that transformation, to spark or
catalyze that transformation, and to coach and facilitate that transformation to
completion.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Introduction to the Coaching The Conversion Meth... (Completed
05/07/18)
Transcript by Rev.com
Page 3 of 4
It's our job, it's within our job description, to empathize and understand their current
context, reflected back to them as accurately and specifically as possible, which in turn
earns us their trust and the authority to invite them to reshift and rethink their thoughts
and attitudes and beliefs in a way that leads them to being more empowered to
purchase your product.
So therefore a sale is so much less about a one-size-fits-all template or a pushy
persuasion tactic and so much more about flexing some very real and genuine empathy
jumps and coaching your lead to the point where they're empowered enough to be a
successful buyer. And when you do this in a strategic way with a clear methodology like
I'm going to show you in the coming modules, you're marketing truly does become a
gift. It's a gift that can release your prospect from old wounds, from doubts, from fears
around the topic or the thing they're trying to sell, and it truly does give them a chance
to feel heard and understood more deeply than ever before. But most of all it gives
them a chance. It gives them a new sense of optimism and hope and chance to evolve in
a direction that matters to them and express themselves in new meaningful ways that
make your product the natural solution. And that is why marketing can be an ultimate
win-win.
At its heart, marketing is a catalyst for change. And for anyone who ever thought
copywriting was a scary esoteric thing where you had to memorize 100 different
triggers, formulas, templates, I invite you right now to just drop the term copywriting
completely and instead just simply learn how to coach the conversion, to give your
customer the gifts of intimacy, of understanding and evolution, and be that trusted
authority figure in their life who can successfully catalyze and lead it.
So in the rest of this module we're going to cover the key principles that really set the
foundation for you becoming a successful conversion coach, and then later in the
modules you'll learn very specifically how to apply those principles and those
characteristics across every phase of your launch or evergreen funnel, particularly within
your email sequences so that you can generate more sales while building greater trust
and intimacy with your audience.
So that's what we have in store, but before we move on I want to leave you with this
image. This is called Japanese joinery art, and there's a link below on this slide or in this
video where you can actually see a video that went viral a little back on Facebook of this
woodworking technique where these pieces just slide together. And it got shared like
crazy, probably because of how satisfying it is to see two complex, intricate, and
nuanced objects just seamlessly click into place. And I want you to imagine your
customer as one of those blocks and your product as the other and you're marketing as
this invisible but powerful force that effortlessly brings and binds them together.
So traditional persuasion and pushy sales techniques which really try to just jam and
force them together, which inevitably would break one, and at the very least create
intense friction, so at best, forcing an unsustainable long-term fit, but when your
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

marketing can act as a force that shaves both pieces, carves the beliefs and chisels away
at the resistance over the course of your funnel, you get this perfect effortless fit.
So those carvings are really the beliefs you need to chisel away with over the course of
your funnel. Your marketing, your messaging and the sequences that drive them, it's
really all about just engineering, engineering that context for that natural conversion to
occur. So it's really where we set up the conditions and circumstances in which your
offer naturally syncs up and connects to your customers' needs and desires
harmoniously.
So with that in mind, I'm going to meet you in less than two where we'll go a bit deeper
into the process of coaching the conversion and a few other of the key concepts that
really set the foundation. I'll see you there.
